opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I keep getting these sharp, quick pains in the left side of my chest, and my heart feels like it's fluttering and beating too hard.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: These episodes keep coming back and I'm really scared it's a heart attack — I can't sleep or relax anymore.

symptom_narrative.onset: started about a month ago and keeps happening in short episodes
symptom_narrative.duration: each pain is brief and quick; the episodes have recurred over the last month
symptom_narrative.character: sharp, quick, stabbing pains on the left side of my chest, and a fluttering, pounding feeling in my heart
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the pain isn't unbearable but it frightens me — it feels like something is wrong with my heart
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: comes and goes; worse when I'm anxious or lying quietly in bed at night
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing really — it usually just passes on its own after a bit
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: feeling anxious, and lying still and quiet so I notice my heartbeat
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: palpitations and being very aware of my heartbeat, feeling generally tired, getting a bit worn out with exercise, and panicky/anxious feelings during the episodes. I haven't fainted, I don't get really breathless, and the pain doesn't spread to my jaw or arm.
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: it keeps happening — several times over the past month
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can still do everything, but I'm holding back because I'm frightened I'll have a heart attack, and it's wrecking my sleep

social_history_volunteered.diet_and_exercise: drinks coffee/caffeine through the day; not doing much regular exercise lately
social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: very anxious about my heart at the moment — it's disrupting my sleep and day-to-day life
